obs_items.label: Capacity purchase agreements with third-party regional carriers (e.g., Republic)
obs_items.notes: Capacity purchase agreements with third-party regional carriers are take-or-pay style commitments; the lease component is capitalized within operating/finance leases but the non-lease (crew/maintenance) service commitments represent ongoing contractual obligations. No aggregate future minimum commitment dollar amount is disclosed in the provided text (only annual expense of $658M under Republic CPA in 2025), so the incremental off-balance-sheet amount cannot be quantified; recorded with null amount and excluded from bridge.

obs_items.label: AAG parent guarantee of American's Credit Facilities and Senior Secured Notes
obs_items.notes: AAG guarantees American's obligations under the Credit Facilities and the 7.25%/8.50% Senior Secured Notes. Because American is a consolidated wholly-owned subsidiary, the guaranteed debt is already reflected in consolidated reported long-term debt; the guarantee is not incremental at the consolidated level. Recorded with null amount, excluded from bridge.

obs_items.label: Litigation reserve adjustments (special items)

citation.quote: Litigation reserve adjustments $77 $— $—

amount.note: A $77M litigation reserve adjustment was recognized in special items in 2025. This is an accrued/expensed reserve already reflected in the financial statements (within other accrued liabilities), not an incremental off-balance-sheet debt-like claim, and it is a P&L adjustment rather than a stated balance-sheet contingent amount. Excluded from bridge.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no leverage-ratio maintenance covenant); this secured facility instead relies on a Collateral Coverage Ratio maintenance covenant (Section 6.09) and a Liquidity covenant (Section 6.08)
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage ratio. Collateral Coverage Ratio maintenance test: a "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" occurs if the ratio falls below 1.6 to 1.0 (Appraised Value of Collateral / Total Obligations)
ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary loss and net loss on Disposition of assets
Foreign currency translation losses (including Indebtedness remeasurement)
Depreciation, amortization (including intangibles) and other non-cash charges/expenses
Net loss on sale/exchange/disposition of capital assets
Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions/mergers/dispositions/debt or equity issuances/investments
Non-cash items (other than accrual of revenue in Ordinary Course) that increased Consolidated Net Income (minus)
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.05 provides for "Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries." Consolidated Net Income and Consolidated EBITDAR exclude the net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, confirming the restricted/unrestricted subsidiary architecture; specific designation conditions/caps are not reproduced in the provided text.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This document is the Eleventh Amendment establishing $362,833,333.30 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments and $155,000,000 of LC Commitments, replacing terminated Existing Revolving/LC Commitments; it is not a new LME transaction. Notable feature: Section Three contains a broad pre-wired consent/authorization mechanism — the 2026 Incremental Revolving Lenders irrevocably consent to and authorize the Administrative Agent, Borrower and Guarantor to enter a future "Specified Amendment" implementing "Specified Terms" without further lender consent, effective upon Required Lenders consenting or, if earlier, upon repayment/repricing/replacement of the 2024 Replacement Term Loans; this reduces individual-lender control and is a coordination/uptier-type flexibility. This is a secured airline facility (SGR/route/slot/gate collateral) protected by a 1.6:1.0 Collateral Coverage Ratio and a Liquidity covenant rather than by a leverage covenant; priming risk is mitigated by the collateral-coverage maintenance test but drop-down/uptier protections are not evident in the excerpted text. J.Crew blocker and MFN sunset provisions are not addressed in the provided sections.

citation.quote: "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.

covenants.leverage_covenant_type: None (no total-leverage maintenance financial covenant); the deal instead uses a Collateral Coverage Ratio maintenance test and a Liquidity covenant
covenants.leverage_ratio_threshold: No leverage ratio. Maintenance tests are: Collateral Coverage Ratio (Section 6.09) with a Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure defined as failure to be at least 1.6 to 1.0; plus a minimum Liquidity covenant (Section 6.08) — dollar threshold not stated in the excerpts provided.
ebitda_addback_categories: Extraordinary loss / net loss on dispositions of assets
Foreign currency translation losses (including on Indebtedness remeasurement)
Depreciation and amortization (including intangibles) and other non-cash charges/expenses
Net loss on sale/exchange/disposition of capital assets
Expenses/charges covered by indemnification or reimbursement in connection with permitted acquisitions, mergers, dispositions, debt/equity issuances or investments
covenants.restricted_payments_basket_size: Not specified in the excerpts (Section 6.01 Restricted Payments referenced but basket amounts not reproduced in the provided text).
covenants.mfn_sunset_period: Not present in the provided excerpts. The agreement defines "All-In/Initial Yield" (relevant to MFN pricing on Incremental Term Loans referencing the Original Term Loans' Adjusted Term SOFR floor), but no MFN spread cushion or sunset period is stated in the text provided.
covenants.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Section 5.05 provides for "Designation of Restricted and Unrestricted Subsidiaries" and Consolidated Net Income excludes the net income (loss) of any Unrestricted Subsidiary, confirming an unrestricted-subsidiary construct exists. The specific designation mechanics/limitations are not reproduced in the provided excerpts.
covenants.lme_risk_notes: This is a secured airline financing (American Airlines) collateralized by route authorities, slots, foreign gate leaseholds, aircraft/spare engines, ground equipment, spare parts and related assets held by a Collateral Trustee. Rather than a leverage covenant, credit protection runs through the Collateral Coverage Ratio (must stay ≥ 1.6:1.0) plus a Liquidity covenant and Collateral disposition controls (Section 6.04). The presence of an Unrestricted Subsidiary designation construct (Section 5.05) plus CNI exclusion of Unrestricted Subsidiary results creates potential drop-down/collateral-leakage capacity typical of J.Crew-style risk — though the excerpts do not reproduce a specific IP/asset-transfer blocker, so the strength of any drop-down protection cannot be confirmed from the text provided. The Fourth Amendment itself terminates existing revolver/LC commitments and establishes $1,451,333,333.35 of 2026 Incremental Revolving Commitments; incremental capacity (Section 2.27) is a priming/dilution vector. Uptier risk turns on amendment/voting provisions not included in these excerpts.

citation.quote: "Collateral Coverage Ratio Failure" shall mean, as of any date of determination, the failure of the Collateral Coverage Ratio as of such date to be at least equal to 1.6 to 1.0.
